figures.caption: Figure 1: An overview of the proposed BWAtt. (a) illustrates the overall architecture of the BWAtt network; (b) shows the SPD modeling module; and (c) presents the SPD self-attention mechanism based on Bures-Wasserstein geometry.
Table 3: Average performance (± standard deviation) over 10 runs, comparing GBWAtt with SOTA methods on three EEG datasets.
Table 5: Component-wise ablations on MAMEM and ERN.
Figure 3: Heatmaps of GBWAtt for the S3 subject across four motor-imagery classes on the BCIC-IV-2a dataset. The x-axis and y-axis represent time and EEG channels, respectively.
Figure 5: Heatmaps of GBWAtt for the S11 subject across five different frequencies on the MAMEM-SSVEP-II dataset. The x-axis and y-axis represent time and EEG channels, respectively.
Figure 7: The heatmaps and the visualization result of GBWAtt for two classes on the BCI-ERN datasets S7 model. The x-axis represents time, and the y-axis represents EEG channels.
